---
title: 4 Benchmarking Performance
author:
  name: Adriano Rutz
  orcid: 0000-0003-0443-9902
citation:
  url: https://taxonomicallyinformedannotation.github.io/tima/vignettes/articles/IV-benchmarking.html
comments:
  giscus:
    repo: taxonomicallyinformedannotation/tima
  hypothesis:
    showHighlights: always
creative_commons: CC BY
date: today
format:
  html:
    smooth-scroll: true
google-scholar: true
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: "#>"
lang: en
license: CC BY
opengraph:
  image:
    src: https://github.com/taxonomicallyinformedannotation/tima/blob/main/man/figures/logo.svg
    alt: Taxonomically Informed Metabolite Annotation
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{4 Benchmarking Performance}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
resource_files:
  - benchmark_full_pos.svg
  - benchmark_zoomed_pos.svg
  - benchmark_comparison_pos.svg
  - benchmark_full_neg.svg
  - benchmark_zoomed_neg.svg
  - benchmark_comparison_neg.svg
---

This vignette summarizes benchmark outputs for TIMA and clarifies what is being measured.

The benchmarking dataset was built using <https://zenodo.org/record/11566051>.

It contained positive and negative MS^2^ spectra of multiple ion species ([M+H]^+^, [M+Na]^+^, [M+H~4~N]^+^, ...) coming from different mass spectrometers.

In positive mode,
It was filtered to 27,468 spectra, representing 17,822 structures without stereo.
Of those, only 15,005 spectra (54.0%) corresponded to structures present in the libraries we used to annotate (except GNPS itself).

In negative mode,
It was filtered to 12,060 spectra, representing 9,112 structures without stereo.
Of those, only 6,282 spectra (52.1%) corresponded to structures present in the libraries we used to annotate  (except GNPS itself).

## Evaluation notes

- The curves below are **top-k hit-rate** curves.
- Positive class is exact InChIKey connectivity-layer recovery.
- The benchmark includes out-of-library structures, so we report both:
  - all-query behavior (realistic throughput), and
  - in-library behavior (recoverable subset).
- MCC-based summaries are produced by the targets pipeline in:
  - `data/interim/benchmark/benchmark_metrics_pos.tsv.gz`
  - `data/interim/benchmark/benchmark_metrics_neg.tsv.gz`

## Best 100 candidates
### Positive

![Benchmark top-k hit-rate curves (positive mode)](./benchmark_full_pos.svg)\

### Negative

![Benchmark top-k hit-rate curves (negative mode)](./benchmark_full_neg.svg)\

## Best 25 candidates (zoomed)
### Positive

![Benchmark top-k hit-rate curves zoomed on the first 25 candidates (positive mode)](./benchmark_zoomed_pos.svg)\

### Negative

![Benchmark top-k hit-rate curves zoomed on the first 25 candidates (negative mode)](./benchmark_zoomed_neg.svg)\

## Candidates distribution
### Positive

![Benchmark comparison (positive mode)](./benchmark_comparison_pos.svg)\

### Negative

![Benchmark comparison (negative mode)](./benchmark_comparison_neg.svg)\

## MCC snapshot from targets outputs

Top-k rows in the metrics snapshot are now interpreted at the candidate-row
level: `tp/tn/fp/fn` are computed from whether each candidate is inside the
top-*k* window and whether it is the correct answer.

```{r}
read_metrics <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  utils::read.delim(path, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

metrics_pos <- read_metrics("~/.tima/data/interim/benchmark/benchmark_metrics_pos.tsv.gz")
metrics_neg <- read_metrics("~/.tima/data/interim/benchmark/benchmark_metrics_neg.tsv.gz")

print_mcc <- function(df, mode_label) {
  if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0L) {
    cat(sprintf("%s: metrics file not available in this build context.\n", mode_label))
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }

  cat(sprintf("\n%s: top-k MCC\n", mode_label))
  print(subset(df, metric_family == "top_k", select = c(prediction, k, mcc, hit_rate_all, hit_rate_truth)))

  cat(sprintf("\n%s: score-threshold MCC\n", mode_label))
  print(subset(df, metric_family == "score_threshold", select = c(prediction, threshold, mcc, tp, tn, fp, fn)))
}

print_mcc(metrics_pos, "Positive mode")
print_mcc(metrics_neg, "Negative mode")
```
